clean_body: | open for a VEEP gif! Peter — We hope you’ve had a great start to 2021! It’s hard to believe February 1st is right around the corner. As we look toward next month, we shift our focus to campaign planning. Thought out, solid plans are essential to a successful campaign, and as 2020 taught us, being ready to adapt those plans is crucial! Thankfully, we KNOW you’re a lot quicker on your feet than these three: | With that in mind — if you follow our February programming, by the end of the month you’ll have built communication, digital, field, and fundraising plans for your campaign! And you’ll be prepared to adapt those plans as needed. Amazing, right? Let’s get planning! — The NDTC Team Please note: Our virtual offices will be closed on February 1. Virtual Live Trainings the Week of February 1 Friday, February 5 at 1pm ET Asking “Why” is a great place to start a project. This is why we are kicking off our planning series of trainings by walking you through the “Whys” of setting goals, having plans, and having a cohesive strategy are key to a successful campaign. New Blog Posts **by Isaac Chasen In order to stay engaged this off-year, we must take stock of what Democrats have recently accomplished... by Emily Dao The main goal of your campaign is to win. If people don’t know who you are, you’re simply not going... 30-Day Challenge Great news — You can now access our 30-Day Challenge for potential candidates whenever you want! This is a great opportunity if you missed your chance earlier this month to be a part of this amazing program designed to help you get ready to run for office. Online Academy Course Spotlight Jan Gaetjens By making the choice to run for office or support a candidate who is running, you’ve made the choice to play an active and visible role in shaping the culture of American politics. This course will help orient you to this landscape and introduce ways in which you can be a positive actor and contributor to conversations about equity and justice in political organizing. | This message was sent to: | Connect with us on social media!** | | | ---|---|--- | ---|--- Paid for by the National Democratic Training Committee, TrainDemocrats.org, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 910 17th Street NW, Suite 925, Washington, DC 20006

name: Team Ro
subject: Not a penny from corporate PACs
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[1]Ro Khanna

The reality
is not many members of Congress refuse to take money from corporate PACs.
It makes fundraising more difficult because it requires earning
small-dollar donations from everyday working people, instead of relying on
lobbyist checks.

But, our team refuses to accept corporate PAC money. Not a single penny.
Even with the challenges it poses, we think this is the right way to
represent the people.

That’s why 100% of Ro’s donations are from his constituents and working
families. They are the people our team is fighting for and who Ro listens
to when it comes to writing and passing legislation.

But it also means we need to ask more often than we would like for your
support.

[ https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5153?refcode=ema-1611&t=1020&akid=1611%2E946728%2EGVNCBr ]Can you make a donation today towards our goal of $15,000 by the end
of January? It’s donations from people like you who keep our campaign
running day after day.

[ https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5153?refcode=em-1611&amounts=&t=1021&akid=1611%2E946728%2EGVNCBr ]Contribute

See, when you donate to a campaign like ours, you’re telling the
establishment that you are ready for change. That you don’t care which
rich and powerful person made the rules, and that you’re ready to break
them. Because when the rules no longer help everyday working families,
they aren’t just rules – they’re barriers.

Every day, Ro goes to work to fight to tear down the barriers that are
stopping folks from receiving comprehensive health care without debt, or
are forcing families to work multiple jobs just to feed their kids. And
your support helps him focus on that work.

[ https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5153?refcode=em-1611&t=1022&akid=1611%2E946728%2EGVNCBr ]By making a donation today – and helping us hit our January goal – you
are supporting the grassroots change that will make our country work for
all of us. Please, chip in what you can to show the corporate PACs and
establishment that they don’t own Ro.

Thank you for your endless support,

Team Ro

Contribute to Ro's campaign for re-election: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ro-khanna?refcode=em20210129footer&t=1023&refcode2=1611_946728_GVNCBr&akid=1611%2E946728%2EGVNCBr

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.rokhanna.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=1611%2E946728%2EGVNCBr

PAID FOR BY RO FOR CONGRESS
PO Box 3513, Santa Clara, CA 95051

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Committee Assignments for 2021 📁
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
TIMtalk will keep you updated on the important issues and goings-on in our campaign, in Tennessee, and in our country. [](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=b2a4e927ea584436aad0d0e8a31a458b&_e=1BecIIg9bG3c9SMAZthjfYbVFrAxy7tW2T0wSSq7tZEIc9jsuCrtuAbp-Ao2gOjek-07YAgngG6MqS6t_9QY6Koa8SB5YzJuycF3cgyWDHsETb8idPC-Hpgxqxz6rriIcjUFd8gSnNF8vMACnEzURfg6E5K-8BQrmXJ5yteZ2sw15osofjLCtpJ_0FQfsUjCiYpoM1F1Jxuvy18_X1_a_i_UtHwhFi_jbgsrk4X5O3F3iMoldFlzjBShTLeMjQYGyKC3SQb5vYoRVyrhLyIiLEKqb57v9oyZ3tDmiVLdce8%3D) **Committee Assignments for 2021 📁
** **Big news! I’m proud to have been assigned to the House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee** , which focuses on federal policy for road, water, rail and air transportation. My membership on the committee will be a great way for me to fight for East Tennessee’s transportation and infrastructure needs. I’m looking forward to strengthening our regional assets and better meeting the energy needs of the southeast.
Of course, in this role I will also be able to promote conservative transportation policies that prioritize responsible solutions to the problems facing America’s transport and infrastructure network. As with everything, I will stand against wasteful spending and search for commonsense solutions.
I will also be returning to the House Foreign Affairs Committee, where I will continue focusing on the growing threats from Communist China and Iran, supporting Israel, and looking out for our military members. | |
---
**#NeverForget 🙏
**
This week marked International Holocaust Remembrance Day—**a day commemorating the 6 million Jewish lives murdered by the Nazis ... a day for the world to renew our vow to never let such an atrocity happen again**. Throughout my career I’ve worked hard to make sure Americans are educated about the Holocaust and emphasized the need to call out anti-Semitism wherever it comes from.
** **
In 2005, I headed the project to build a [Holocaust memorial](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=b2a4e927ea584436aad0d0e8a31a458b&_e=1BecIIg9bG3c9SMAZthjfRjpuM9GLvqlVH4XD4399krUzZOHux0MXOvhuvN0loFbSeisA2ZzPxf2UpAZAwZJhAalUCkBniRODsV4Sp9MWCULkRWQ3Y2eOLMcEq7pFuGRruDR_4rv1B7xlVy2MGm9fyq5LAF2ft4toGsNpPCLw7a50AsQlrEkahuAXSFX_Os0ZkccuAVrwameP_t9UnKNqKDe0YDcEvLpQKZQRgm3Go4goJRzq3fuUfaGj_DWZ7P2vhpVprhegWUqD9TuZigbjR6f7N0sOGGwlYSjULG0UjU28tBN9rqM8zEu-nwnE2Bq3vSEipAs64AKQyZfYJUeBiSeJ-TV2ovi6_p5z55F3PFnuyJMkjZLoXTOQMm3t8s2) in West Hills / John Bynon Park. I built it myself with the help of my parents. It features six cedar trees representing the 6 million murdered Jews. A large stone inscribed with Hebrew text rests in the center. This was my way of showing that Knoxville will #neverforget.
|
---
**Just for Fun 🐔
**
_Photo courtesy of KHOU 11 News
_

When a computer glitch caused a traffic jam at a COVID vaccine clinic in South Carolina, the mayor had no choice but to “[call the pros](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=b2a4e927ea584436aad0d0e8a31a458b&_e=1BecIIg9bG3c9SMAZthjfUY3rdyjp5vGGHVF3MXovaHxYGi-yn-FOMyMoJ65MaCz-afhaIlRToFhvP-DYQfaJsTWuU07VgF61VGmJAcK4IZ2t_kYpZWxz92Z0aC_j_aAF2UTmHXDEf4wgbRoNHLvykFE5S6BUn-kNyJuhJaNMtywKtbhbr8lygALHyzMvXi-DuH7lE8-4ms699TojuUjo9kbfABza06dUe-hfH9WuXUFq8dqWfNF-fmubeiKvn-Yhy6eIZkDnQsTAtlgAQyP0Z_jt9OdZsakCfG-pq7qy5T6j6vOzhQSFn_19GZnadjlbaOee1NS-KOxN4Tckv0leATXsGXokaJiTJFPHxAVTZAJC-rtLDK0hJe5NM_7qgpk).” **And by pros, I mean Chick-fil-A, of course**. Mount Pleasant Mayor Will Haynie asked Chick-fil-A manager Jerry Walkowiak to draw on his expertise and get the traffic moving. Walkowiak was able to get the wait time down from one hour to 15 minutes, after reorganizing and enlisting backup. Now we know where to go to get a good, fast meal!
|
